name: VoltAgent
tagline: Open-source TypeScript framework for building and running AI agents in production
metaDescription: VoltAgent is an open-source TypeScript framework for building AI agents with memory, tools, RAG, voice, and multi-step workflows, plus a console for observability.
overview: VoltAgent is an open-source TypeScript framework for building AI agents that you can ship to production. You define agents in code with typed roles, tools, memory, and a model provider, then connect them to multi-step workflows and teams of specialized sub-agents that work together under a supervisor.

The framework is provider-agnostic, so you can switch between OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and other models by changing configuration instead of rewriting agent logic. It pairs with the VoltOps console, available as a cloud or self-hosted service, which gives you observability, evals, guardrails, and deployment tooling for the agents you build.
features: Core runtime (@voltagent/core) to define agents with typed roles, tools, memory, and model providers in one place
Declarative workflow engine for describing multi-step automations, including human-in-the-loop suspend and resume
Supervisor and sub-agent runtime for coordinating teams of specialized agents on a single task
Zod-typed tool registry plus Model Context Protocol (MCP) support to connect external servers without extra glue code
Built-in memory adapters, retrieval and RAG, voice (text-to-speech and speech-to-text), guardrails, and agent evals
VoltOps console (cloud or self-hosted) for observability, tracing, prompts, and deployment of your agents

gettingStarted.intro: VoltAgent ships as npm packages with a project generator. The CLI scaffolds a starter project that registers both an agent and a workflow example, then runs a local server you can connect to from the VoltOps console.

steps.heading: Create a new project
steps.body: Use the create-voltagent-app CLI to scaffold a project. The command walks you through setup and generates starter code in src/index.ts.

steps.heading: Define an agent
steps.body: In the generated code you create an Agent with instructions, a model provider, and tools, then register it with VoltAgent. You can attach persistent memory using an adapter such as LibSQL.

steps.heading: Run the dev server
steps.body: Start the project from your terminal. The VoltAgent server starts an HTTP endpoint (default http://localhost:3141) that you can open in the VoltOps console to chat with and inspect your agent.

useCases: Building production AI agents in TypeScript with code-level control over tools, memory, and model providers
Coordinating multi-agent systems where specialized sub-agents work together under a supervisor
Automating multi-step processes, including human-in-the-loop flows that can suspend and resume, with the workflow engine
Grounding agent responses on your own data using built-in retrieval and RAG, then monitoring runs in the VoltOps console
